facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: South Third crossing under full district seal, beside the barricaded street fissure over the exposed subsurface void/bell-foundation structure

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Unranked, unlicensed salvage runner; shoulder in sling and sharply aggravated again by crouching and repeated strain, with headache worsened by deep listening; provisional notice and pending deposition still active; now has direct listening confirmation of cautious movement below, pulsed/interrupted venting that suggests passage near the air throat, visible proof of smooth old masonry under the street, and a stronger theory that another interior access route exists beyond the street crack

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Use the brief window before morning to keep the restricted site record from being captured by Pike or Review, preserve Pell as the first-report channel, and decide how to act on the likely alternate access route without surrendering it in the street

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Rowan still has no license, sponsor, money cushion, or lawful authority at the cordon; Maelin has sealed the lane and forbidden descent tonight; Bren remains openly hostile and will escalate through contractor and desk channels; Review has now arrived in person with Hadrik; Rowan is concealing probable knowledge about another route while under increasing scrutiny

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: Lio remains Rowan’s public ally and backs him against Bren and Review pressure; Pell is now a wary but active procedural buffer who bought Rowan a few hours and remains the preferred reporting channel; Mara Arl stays in uneasy alignment, more distrustful after catching Rowan’s partial lie but still committed to Benn’s truth; Nessa remains an off-site archival ally through the bell-foundation trace; Maelin becomes a hard-edged conditional authority contact who does not trust Rowan but validates enough danger to block contractor control

facts.subject: protagonist
facts.object: The South Third fight has shifted from proving anomaly to controlling response: district authority now accepts a restricted live-hazard interpretation, possible movement below and badly reset markers raise the chance of survivors or intruders, and Rowan must navigate a three-way struggle among Pike interests, district process, and Hall Review before the buried structure, missing men, and any alternate entrance are seized or suppressed
